text: Document level sentiment classification is still a hard task due to the reason that it needs to capture the inner relations between sentences and keep track of the semantic meaning of a whole document. In this paper, we design a neural network model, called GNSD (Gated Network for Sentiment Documentation), to generate a vector representation for a document in a unified bottom-up way. It first uses a convolutional neural network or long short-term memory to extract the sentence representation for each sentence in a document. Then it utilizes a gated recurrent neural network to generate the document representation while capturing the semantic meaning of the sentences and their relationships. Experiments for document level sentiment classification are performed on four large-scale review datasets from IMDB and Yelp Dataset Challenge, and demonstrate that the proposed GNSD model outperforms the state-of-the-art approaches, and that the Gated Recurrent Neural Network (GRNN) is much better than the standard Recurrent Neural Network (RNN) in the GNSD model for document level sentiment classification. 1
